senses.definition: a document that allows someone to practice driving under supervision before obtaining a full driver's license
a document that proves we can drive a car
a permit that allows individuals to legally operate large vehicles such as trucks and buses for commercial purposes
a booklet that provides essential information and guidelines for operating a motor vehicle safely and legally
a booklet that provides instructions and guidance for operating and maintaining a specific product or device
a written agreement in which a manufacturer promises a customer to repair or replace a product, under certain conditions, within a specific period of time

examples.x: The car came with a three-year warranty, so I don’t need to worry if something breaks down.
I still have a warranty on my phone, so I can get it fixed for free if it stops working.
Make sure to keep the receipt; without it, you cannot claim the warranty.
The washing machine broke after six months, but luckily, it's covered by the warranty.
The warranty on my laptop expired last month, so I’ll have to pay for repairs now.

senses.definition: a document or permit that grants authorization, often for entry or passage

examples.x: He showed his annual pass to enter the national park.
They issued a temporary pass for visitors to the construction site.
The city pass provided discounted admission to museums and attractions.

senses.definition: a fee charged for using a bridge, road, or tunnel
a small booth or structure at a toll plaza where tolls are collected from drivers

examples.x: The tollbooth was unmanned, and drivers had to pay using an automated system.
He waited in line at the tollbooth to pay the toll fee.
They installed new technology in the tollbooth for electronic toll collection.
The tollbooth was equipped with a barrier that lifted after payment.

senses.definition: a designated area on a road or highway where vehicles must pay a fee or toll

examples.x: We stopped at the toll plaza to pay the toll before continuing on the highway.
The toll plaza was congested with vehicles during rush hour.
The toll plaza marked the entrance to the expressway.
They installed additional lanes at the toll plaza to reduce traffic delays.
He drove through the toll plaza using an electronic transponder for quick payment.

senses.definition: a fee imposed by the government for owning and using a vehicle on public roads

examples.x: In many countries, road tax must be paid annually to legally drive a car on the streets.
The amount of road tax you pay often depends on factors like the type and engine size of your vehicle.
Failure to pay road tax can result in fines or penalties from transportation authorities.
Some governments offer discounts on road tax for eco-friendly vehicles that produce low emissions.
Road tax revenue is often used to maintain and improve the infrastructure of roads and highways.

senses.definition: a fee charged for using roads to manage traffic flow and reduce congestion

examples.x: Road pricing aims to decrease traffic jams during rush hours by making drivers consider alternative routes or times.
Some cities have implemented road pricing to encourage public transportation use and reduce air pollution.
The effectiveness of road pricing varies depending on how well it integrates with existing transportation systems.
Critics argue that road pricing may disproportionately affect lower-income drivers who rely on personal vehicles.
Governments often study the impact of road pricing before implementing it to ensure it benefits both commuters and the environment.

senses.definition: a fee charged to vehicles for entering certain busy areas during peak times, aiming to reduce traffic congestion and improve air quality

examples.x: Some cities have implemented congestion pricing to encourage drivers to use public transport or drive during less busy hours.
The effectiveness of congestion pricing depends on how well it manages traffic flow in high-demand areas.
Residents often debate whether congestion pricing unfairly targets those who need to travel by car for work or other essential reasons.
Many supporters argue that congestion pricing could also reduce greenhouse gas emissions by decreasing the number of cars on the road.
Implementing congestion pricing requires careful planning and consideration of its impacts on various socioeconomic groups within the community.

senses.definition: a system where vehicles are charged electronically while moving through toll stations without needing to stop

examples.x: Open road tolling allows drivers to pass through toll booths without stopping, using electronic devices to pay automatically.
Many modern highways implement open road tolling to reduce traffic congestion and travel time for commuters.
The main advantage of open road tolling is its efficiency in collecting tolls without causing delays for drivers.
Electronic transponders installed in vehicles enable open road tolling systems to deduct fees seamlessly as cars pass through designated points.
Governments often promote open road tolling as a way to streamline traffic flow and enhance the overall driving experience on major roads.

senses.definition: a system where drivers pay a predetermined fee before entering a highway or bridge

examples.x: Closed toll collection ensures that all motorists pay a set fee before using certain roads.
In closed toll collection systems, drivers receive a ticket upon entry and pay when exiting the toll road.
Closed toll collection reduces traffic congestion at toll plazas compared to open toll systems.
Drivers appreciate closed toll collection for its straightforward payment structure and efficiency.
Closed toll collection facilitates smoother traffic flow by minimizing stops and delays at toll booths.

senses.definition: a system that automatically charges drivers for using highways or bridges through electronic means, without requiring them to stop

examples.x: Electronic toll collection allows drivers to pass through toll booths quickly and efficiently, minimizing traffic congestion.
Many countries have adopted electronic toll collection to streamline the payment process and improve road management.
Users of electronic toll collection systems typically have a small electronic device installed in their vehicles.
Electronic toll collection systems are designed to enhance convenience and reduce the need for cash transactions on highways.
Electronic toll collection systems are becoming increasingly popular worldwide due to their ability to enhance traffic flow and reduce environmental impacts associated with idling at toll booths.

senses.definition: a system where tolls are collected electronically without accepting cash payments at toll booths

examples.x: Many highways now use cashless tolling to keep traffic moving smoothly and to make paying tolls easier for drivers.
With cashless tolling, drivers either have a device in their car that automatically pays the toll, or their license plate is scanned.
The main benefit of cashless tolling is that it saves time for drivers because they don't have to stop to pay their tolls.
Governments are encouraging cashless tolling to upgrade roads and bridges and to make travel more efficient for everyone.
The transition to cashless tolling aims to modernize infrastructure and reduce operational costs associated with traditional toll collection methods.

senses.definition: a road used to avoid paying tolls on a toll road

examples.x: During peak travel seasons, many drivers opt for the shunpike to save money on tolls.
We decided to take the shunpike to avoid the long lines at the toll plaza.
Local residents often use the shunpike to avoid the tolls imposed on the main expressway.
The family always took the shunpike to avoid the hefty toll charges on the main highway.
The truck drivers preferred the shunpike route to save on toll expenses during their long hauls.

senses.definition: the amount of money we pay to travel with a bus, taxi, plane, etc.

examples.x: She bought a monthly pass to save on daily fare expenses.
She checked the fare on the ride-hailing app before confirming her trip.
The airline offers lower fares for early morning flights.
The fare from the city to the beach was quite reasonable.

senses.definition: a charge levied on the detention of a vessel, container, or cargo beyond the allotted time for loading or unloading at a port

examples.x: The shipping company incurred demurrage fees due to delays in unloading the cargo from the vessel.
The importer was frustrated by the unexpected demurrage charges resulting from delays in clearing customs.
To avoid demurrage costs, the logistics manager ensured efficient coordination between the warehouse and transportation teams.
The port authority implemented demurrage penalties to incentivize prompt loading and unloading of vessels.
Despite the efforts to minimize delays, the shipping company occasionally faced demurrage charges when unforeseen circumstances caused disruptions in their operations.

senses.definition: given or offered free of charge as a gesture of goodwill or favor

examples.x: The repair shop provided courtesy car rentals to customers whose vehicles were undergoing repairs.
As a courtesy service, the clinic provided free initial consultations to new patients.
The airline provided courtesy meals on the delayed flight as compensation for the inconvenience.
The conference venue included courtesy parking for attendees during the event.
